def: s. A wall.

ety: an. inf. n. of حيي 'to live'
def: s.m. An animal, a beast, brute; — (fig̠.) a stupid fellow, dolt, blockhead
more: n. m. 1. A brute or irrational animal (Opp. of insān man); beast. 2. A fool; an idiot. haivān-i-mutlaq , n. m. An animal; a brute. haivān-i-nāhiq ; H. gadhā , n. m. An ass. haivān-i-nātiq , n. m. Man.
(from حيّ) s. m. An animal, a beast, brute. (Plur. حيوانات).
subs: s.m. 'An absolute animal,' a beast or brute
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian حَیْوَان (haywān), borrowed from Arabic حَيَوَان (ḥayawān).

def: s.m. pl. (of ḥaiwān), Animals, brutes.
adj. Of or relating to an animal or animals; animal, brutish, brutal, beastly, bestial
more: adj. Animal; brutal; bestial. aql-i-haivānī , n. f. The instinct of animals.

ety: for A. حيوانية, abst. subst. fr. ḥaiwān
def: s.f. Animality; animal nature, beastliness, bestiality, brutality (opp. to inṣānīyat, 'humanity').
more: n. f. Brutality, in contradistinction to insāniyat humanity.

def: adj. Having life, living, alive
more: (Heb. ) adj. Alive. حي قيوم ḥaiy ḳaiyūm, adj. Eternal, immortal. حي لايموت ḥaiy lā-yamūt, Eternal.
subs: and ḥaiy-lā-yamūt, adj. Eternal; immortal, imperishable.

def: n. f. 1. State (حال 1). 2. Face; aspect. 3. The aspect of the heavens; astronomy. haiat-i-majmūī , n. f. The general aspect or condition. haiat-i-nikāh , n. f. Coverture.
s.m. A quarter, tract, region, place, part; — a court-yard, an area.
more: (from حاز) s. m. 1. A place, part, a tract of ground. 2. A court-yard, an area.

def: s.f. The menses
more: n. m. The menses; the monthly course. haiz-āvar , n. m. An emmenagogue; medicine to promote menstrual discharge. haiz kā lattā, gaddī , n. m. A pessary. Apnī bāt ko haiz ke latte kī tarah chhupātī hai . Wom. haiz yā kapṛoṅ se h. , v. n. To have the monthly course. valad-ul-haiz, haizī bach hā, haizī , n. m. 1. A bastard. 2. A brat; an imp.
s. f. The menses. ولد الحيض waladu-l-ḥaiẓ, A term of reproach.
subs: s.m. A medicine to promote menstrual discharge, emmenagogue
s.m. = ḥaiẓ-kī gaddī, s.f. A pessary
s.m. A term of reproach; bastard; brat, imp.

def: n. m. Cholera morbus. haizā h. , v. n. To have an attack of cholera. haizā kar-ke marnā . To die of cholera.

ety: for A. هيضة haiẓat, inf. n. of هاض (for هيض
def: s.m. Cholera morbus; flux and vomit together
more: s. m. The cholera morbus, a flux and vomit.
subs: (-ko), To have an attack of cholera.
wety: Borrowed from Arabic هَيْضَة (hayḍa).

def: adj. & s.m. Illegitimate; — wicked, impish; — bastard; brat, imp
more: adj. 1. Illegitimate; unlawfully begotten. 2. Wicked; naughty.

ety: for A. حجلة ḥajalat, fem., fr. حجل 'to interpose an obstacle,' &c.
def: s.m. A kind of curtained canopy or alcove or the like, prepared for a bride; an ornamented pavilion or chamber with curtains, for a bride; the curtain which hangs before her; — the marriage bed.

ety: for A. حجلة ḥajalat, fem.; n. of un. fr., or the fem. of, ḥajal, 'the partridge'; rt. حجل 'to walk with short steps'
def: s.m. A partridge, the female partridge.
more: (vulg. ḥujla ) s. m. 1. The female partridge. 2. The ornamented chamber where the bride sits, the curtain which hangs before her, the marriage-bed.

def: s.f. The craft or art of the ḥajjām, and the operation that he performs; cupping, scarifying; shaving; a shave
more: n. f. 1. The functions of a barber. 2. Shaving; a shave (Sant. hoyo ). 3. Plunder; robbery. Kal miyāṅ hajjām phirte the jo sab ko mūṅḍte, Sho/?/kh ke kūche meṅ unkī bhī hajāmat ban gaī . The barber went about ashaving men but yesterday, In Love lane see his mistress hath him shaven clean today. hajāmat banānā , v. a. 1. To shave. 2. To plunder; rob; cheat. Kore ustre se hajāmat banā dī . He shaved him clean with a new razor (plundered him).
(from حجم He cupped, &c.) s. f. Cupping, scarifying, shaving.
subs: (-kī), To be shaved; — (fig.) to be spoiled or plundered.

ety: v.n. fr. حجر 'to prevent, to resist'
def: s.m. A stone; — prohibition; (in Law) annulment
more: n. حجر hindered. A stone. hajar-ul-asvad , n. m. The black stone in the temple of Mecca. hajar-ul-yahūd , n. m. A calcareous stone used in medicine; Jew's stone, resembling an olive or acorn, found in Palestine.

s. m. A stone. (Plur. احجار aḥjār ). حجرتان ḥajratān, Gold and silver. حجرالاسود ḥajaru-l-aswad, s. m. The black stone in the temple of Mecca. حجراليـﮩود ḥajaru-l-yahūd, s. m. A calcareous stone used in medicine, jewʼs-stone, resembling an olive or acorn, found in Palestine. (Helmintholithus judaicus: Lin. Helmintholithus echini β . a. Gmel. ).
subs: s.m. The black stone of the kaʻba or temple of Mecca
s.m. Jews-stone, a calcareous stone used in medicine (found in Palestine, and resembling an olive or acorn), Helmintholithus judaicus.

def: adj. corr. of هزار hazār, q.v. s.m. Caution, wariness, vigilance, care; prudence; — fear.

ety: for A. حاجة, fr. حاج (for حوج
def: s.f. Want, need, necessity, exigency, poverty; a thing wanted, an object of want or need, a requirement, a needful or requisite thing, affair or business; a call of nature; hope, wish; — a place of detention (for prisoners pending trial)
more: n. f. حوج needed. 1. Requirement; necessity ( ehtiyāj ). 2. A lock-up for prisoners pending trial. 3. A call of nature. hājat-barār , n. m. One who provides what is needed. hājat rafa k. , v. a. 1. To relieve one's necessities; supply a want. 2. To go to the necessary; to relieve nature. hājat-ravāī , n. f. The act of providing what is needed; provision; succour. hājat-mand , adj. Necessitous; indigent; needy. hājat meṅ rakhnā , v. a. To detain in the lock-up.

(from حاج Heb. Moved round, frequented, stood in need of) s. f. 1. Need, want, necessity, poverty. 2. Hope, wish. حاجت برآر ḥājat-bar-ār, Producing what is wanted or desired. حاجت رفع کرنا ḥājat rafﻌ karnā, 1. To relieve oneʼs necessities. 2. To go to the necessary. حاجت مند ḥājat-mand, adj. 1. Necessitous, indigent. 2. Hoping, depending. حاجت روا ḥājat-rawā, adj. Supplying or obtaining what is needed. حاجت رواٴي ḥajat-rawā-ī, s. f. The obtaining what is wanted or needed. (Plur. حاجات).
subs: adj. & s.m. Providing what is wanted or desired; — one who provides what is needed
(-kī or apnī), To relieve (one's) necessities, to supply a want; to go to the necessary, to ease nature
adj. Providing or supplying what is needed
(-kī or apnī), To supply (one's) wants, to provide what is needful or necessary
s.f. The act of providing what is needed, supplying the wants (of), provision, succour
adj. Needy, necessitous, indigent; hoping, depending
v.t. To keep or place (a prisoner) in the house of detention.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian حاجت (hājat), from Arabic حَاجَة (ḥāja).

def: s.f. pl. (of ḥājat), Wants, necessities.
s.m. One in need, a mendicant; a supplicant; — s.f. A chamber utensil.
more: n. 1. A tin chamber utensil. Hājatī meṅ peshāb kartā hūṅ . Gālib. 2. A supplicant; pilgrim. Mendicants. E Qalandar bolī! kar de murādeṅ pūriyāṅ! Des des se hājatī āë, kar-ke bahut kandāriyāṅ . Wom.

def: s.m. Intervening, interposing, precluding, secluding, excluding, fencing round.
more: s. m. Interposing, secluding, excluding, fencing round.

ety: for hazh-dah = hash-dah = hasht + dah, qq.v.
def: adj. Eighteen.
adj. Eighteenth.
n. m. ﮩجو A satirist.

ety: for A. هاجٍ hājiṉ, act. part. of هَجَا (for هجو
def: s.m. A satirist.
more: (part. act. of هجو) s. m. A satirist.

ety: for ḥājjī, a n. of un. fr. ḥājj, q.v.; — not found in classical Arabic, but used by the Persians and Turks
def: s.m. A pilgrim (to Mecca); one who has performed the pilgrimage (of Mecca)
more: n. m. hajjan , n. f. حج he repaired to. One who has made the pilgrimage to Mecca. 1. Makke gaë, na Madīnā gaë, bīch hī bīch hājī bhaë . Prov. Nor Mecca nor Medina reached, midway a Hājī called. 2. Hājī jo haj karte phireṅ, nām-i-Khudā kabhī nā līyā, Dil kā kufar ṭūṭā nahīṅ, Makke gaë tau kyā huā? He pilgrimages frequent makes, God's name he never takes, An infidel in heart, in vain for Mecca's shrine he makes. hājī-ul-harmain , n. m. One who has made a pilgrimage to both the sacred places, Mecca and Medina.

(part. act. from حجّ Heb. He turned round, danced, celebrated a festival; peculiarly, performed the pilgrimage to Mecca) s. m. A pilgrim to Mecca. حاجي الحرمين ḥājiyu-l-ḥaramain, A pilgrim to both Mecca and Medīna. (Plur. حجاج).
subs: ḥājī-bewuqūf, s.m. (local), Two mock pilgrims who play a conspicuous part in the Mǒḥarram festival
s.m. A pilgrim to the two sacred places (Mecca and Medina); one who has performed the pilgrimage of the two sacred places.

ety: act. part. n. of حجب 'to prevent,' &c.
def: s.m. A door-keeper, porter; a chamberlain; a minister of state; — a curtain, screen ( = ḥijāb); — the eye-brow.
more: (from حجب q. v.) s. m. 1. A porter, a door-keeper, a chamberlain. 2. A curtain, a skreen. 3. The eyebrow.
s. m. (dakh.) An attorney at law ( Harris ).

def: adj. & s.m. Hindering, intervening, &c.; — a thing intervening, an obstacle or obstruction, a barrier, &c.

ety: act. part. n. of حجّ 'to repair,' &c.
def: s.m. = ḥājī, q.v. s.m. A pilgrim (to Mecca); one who has performed the pilgrimage (of Mecca)
subs: ḥājī-bewuqūf, s.m. (local), Two mock pilgrims who play a conspicuous part in the Mǒḥarram festival
s.m. A pilgrim to the two sacred places (Mecca and Medina); one who has performed the pilgrimage of the two sacred places.

ety: inf. n. of حج 'to repair to'
def: s.m. Repairing or going (to a place); the act of moving round; — pilgrimage (to Mecca)
more: n. m. حج moving round. The pilgrimage to Mecca. 1. Sattar chūhe khā-ke billī haj ko chalī . Prov. After she had devoured seventy rats the cat went on a pilgrimage. (First sin then prayer). 2. Makke meṅ rahte haiṅ par haj nahīṅ kīyā . Prov. He lives in Mecca and he never visited the pilgrim shrine. (The nearer the church, the farther from God.) 3. Gālib! gar is safar meṅ mujhe sāth le chaleṅ, Haj kā savāb nazr karūṅgā hazūr kī! Gālib. Gālib! if but the king to Mecca would take thee, The Heaven I'd earn I would present his Majesty. hajj-i-asg/?/ar , n. m. The minor pilgrimage to Mecca. hajj-i-akbar , n. m. The greater pilgrimage to Mecca in the Māh-i-zilhij . It consists in making the circuit of the Kābah on a Friday, when the 10th of Bakrā Īd falls on that day. hajj-ul-farz . The enjoined pilgrimage to Mecca, which should be performed once in his lifetime by every good Musalmān.
(Heb. ) s. m. 1. Act of moving round, or going to a place. 2. Pilgrimage to Mecca.
subs: s.m. The enjoined pilgrimage (to Mecca, which it is the duty of every true Musalmān to perform once in his lifetime).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian حَجّ (hajj), from Arabic حَجّ (ḥajj).

def: s.m. A cupper, scarifier, phlebotomist; a shaver, a barber (a barber in the East commonly performs the operation of bleeding, &c.).
more: n. m. حجم cupped. 1. A barber. 2. A barber-surgeon; one who not only shaves, but bleeds, cups, cleans the ears, pares the nails, etc., usually included in the village establishment. 1. Hajjām kā laṛkā pahle ustād hī kā sir mūṅḍtā hai . Prov. The barber's apprentice first practises on his master's head. 2. Hajjām kā ustrā mere sir par bhī phirtā hai, tumhāre sar par bhī . Prov. The same razor shaves us both alike. (I am as good as you). 3. Hajjām ke āge sab kã sir jhuktā hai . Prov. Every one must bow his head to the barber. hajjām kī kisbat . A barber's razor case.
(adj. superl. from حجامت) s. m. A cupper, scarifier, barber, shaver, phlebotomist.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian حَجَّام (hajjām), borrowed from Arabic حَجَّام (ḥajjām).

def: s.f. = ḥajāmat, q.v. s.f. The craft or art of the ḥajjām, and the operation that he performs; cupping, scarifying; shaving; a shave
more: n. f. The profession of a barber.
subs: (-kī), To be shaved; — (fig.) to be spoiled or plundered.

def: s.f. The wife of a barber.
s.f. Thickness, bigness, magnitude, bulk, size (a post-classical signification).

ety: inf. n. of هجم 'to drive in,' &c.
def: s.m. Driving or forcing in; — urging on; — driving off or away; repulsing; — attacking suddenly; assault, onset; — extirpation; — demolition.
more: s. m. 1. Driving in. 2. Repulsing. 3. Attacking suddenly. 4. Extirpation.

ety: fr. hajo, q.v.; — ṛā prob. = S. र+कः
def: s.m. A satirist.
s.m. Separation; disjunction; — desertion (of country or friends); — absence.
more: s. m. Separation, disjunction, desertion of country or friends, absence.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian هَجْر (hajr), borrowed from Arabic هَجْر (hajr), from هَجَرَ (hajara). First attested in c. 1611 as Middle Hindi ہجر (hjr /⁠hajr⁠/). Doublet of ہِجْرَت (hijrat).

ety: dual of ḥajarat, n. of un. fr. ḥajar
def: s.m. Gold and silver.
n. f. 1. A satire; lampoon; pasquinade. 2. Disrepute; infamy. hajv-i-malīh , n. f. Irony.
s.f. Satirizing, &c.; — satire, lampoon, pasquinade; — blame, disrepute, infamy
more: s. f. Satire, a lampoon, a pasquinade. هجومليح hajo-malīḥ, f. Apparent praise but real satire, irony.
s. (dakh.) Satire, pasquinade ( Harris ). هجو کرنا hajo (or dakh. hajū) karnā, To satirize.
subs: s.f. Seeming praise, but real satire; irony.
ex: ماضی میں ہجووں کے نشر پر زیادہ سخت پابندیاں ہوتی تھیں۔
māzī mẽ hajvõ ke naśr par zyāda saxt pābandiyā̃ hotī thī̃.
In the past, there used to be greater restrictions on the publication of lampoons.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian هجو (hajw), from Arabic هَجْو (hajw).

def: s. f. (dakh.) See هانک hānk.
s. (plur. of حقيقت) Truths, narrations, &c.
s.f. pl. (of ḥaqīqat), Truths, realities, &c.
v.t. (dialec.) = hakārnā.
more: v. a. (dakh.) To drive: see هانکنا hānknā.

def: s.m. An umpire, arbitrator, mediator.
more: n. m. حکم commanded. An umpire; arbitrator; a mediator.
s. m. 1. An umpire, a mediator, an arbitrator. 2. (Heb. ) Knowledge, wisdom, &c.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian حُکْم (hukm), borrowed from Arabic حُكْم (ḥukm).

ety: for A. حقارة inf. n. of حقر 'to be contemptible'
def: s.f. Contempt, scorn, disdain; contemptibleness, despicableness, baseness, vileness; — disgrace; affront
more: n. f. حقر was contemptible. Contempt; scorn. hiqārat āyad huī . Exposed to contempt. hiqārat k., haqīr jānnā , v. a. To hold in contempt; to despise. hiqārat kī nazar se dekhnā , v. n. To look down upon. Aë Zauq! kis ko chashm-i-hiqārat se dekhye? Sab ham se haiṅ ziyādah, koī ham se kam nahīṅ . Zauq. O Zauq! whom can you e'er look down upon? All are above you, less than you no one.
(properly ḥaḳārat, from حقر) s. f. Contempt, scorn, disdain, affront, disgrace, vileness, baseness, despicableness.
subs: honā, To be exposed to contempt
(-kī), To treat with contempt, to despise
v.t. To regard with contempt, to look down upon.

def: v.t. = هنکارنا haṅkārnā, q.v. v.n. To call, bawl, halloo; — v.t. To call to, to bawl to; to halloo after (as in the chase); — to brave; — to drive (oxen, &c.); to drive away, to keep off (by shouting); to expel; — to start or set off (a cart, carriage, or train, &c.); — to put off (a boat); to hoist or set (sail, in order to put off).
more: v. a. To drive oxen or other cattle in a circle as when treading out grain.

def: v.t. = haṅkāwnā, q.v.

ety: hak-bak, the weak form of hakkā-bakkā, q.v.+ā = āw = Prk. आव or आवे = S. आपि (caus. aug.) + nā
def: v.n. To be dumb-foundered; to become confused, or irresolute; to be aghast.
more: v. n. To be confused or irresolute (when any thing is to be done), to be aghast.

ety: for A. حقيقة, v.n. fr. حق 'to be just or right,' &c.
def: s.f. Essence (of a thing), essential property or quality; truth, reality, fact, true or real nature or state or circumstances or facts, gist, pith; — rightness, sincerity; — account, narration, relation, story, state, condition, explanation
more: n. f. See asal , 7. 1. The fact; the merits. Ham ko mālūm hai jannat kī haqīqat, lekin Dil ke khush rakhne ko, Gālib, ye khayāl achchā hai . Gālib. Gālib, you know it's all a myth about the heavenly land, Still duth the pleasant cheat believers' hearts with joy expand. 2. Circumstances; case ( hāl . 1). haqīqat-i-hāl ; Pop. haqīqat-hāl , n. f. The facts, circumstances, or merits of a case; statement. haqīqat khulnā , v. n. To be discovered; to appear. Shab-i-vasl-i-udū kyā kyā jalā hūṅ, Haqīqat khul gaī roz-i-jazā kī . Momin. My rival in my mistress' arms—how I did burn all night! Hell fires awaiting judgment I then realized quite. haqīqat likh'nā , v. a. To set down the facts; make a statement; certify or testify to. haqīqat-i-muāmilah . The merits of a case; particulars of a transaction. haqīqat meṅ, fil haqīqat, dar haqīqat , adv. In fact; in truth; in deed; bona-fide ; essentially; v…
(from حقّ) s. f. 1. Truth, rightness, sincerity, reality, fact. 2. Account, narration, relation, story, state, condition, explanation.
subs: or ḥaqīqat-ḥāl, s.f. The true or real facts or circumstances of a case; statement
or dar ḥaqīqat, or fiʼl-ḥaqīqat, adv. In truth, in fact, really, essentially
s.m. A written statement of facts or particulars.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian حَقِیقَت (haqīqat), from Arabic حَقِيقَة (ḥaqīqa). Related to حَق (haq).

ety: for A. حقيقةً, acc. of ḥaqīqat
def: adv. In truth, in reality, truly, really ( = ḥaqīqat-meṅ).
more: adv. Really, truly, in truth, in reality.

def: adj. Essential, genuine; true, real, just, certain, accurate, actual; own
more: adj. 1. Genuine; real; true; bona fide (اصلي 2, 6, 7, 9). 2. Own ( apnā ); whole-blood. haqīqī bhāī ; E. ek lād ke bhaī , Own brother; brother of the full blood; a full brother. Birādar-i-haqīqī dushman-i-mādarzād hai . Prov. One's own brother is one's foe of mother born. haqīqī mālik . The real owner. rishta-i-haqīqī . A relation of the full blood. vārisān-i-haqīqī . Rightful heirs.
adj. Real, true, certain, accurate, just, actual, own. حقيقي بهاٴي ḥaḳīḳī bhāʼī, A full brother.
subs: s.m. A relation of the full blood.

ety: act. part. n. of حکم 'to restrain'; to judge'; 'to exercise rule,' &c.
def: s.m. One who exercises judicial authority, jurisdiction, rule, dominion, or government; a judge; a magistrate; one in power or authority; a ruler, governor, an official, officer; a commander, chief, master; — the Supreme Judge (one of the names of God)
more: n. m. حکم he restrained him. 1. One who exercises, or is intrusted with authority; one empowered; a ruler; governor; chief; master; a magistrate; judge; an officer; the administrative authority in a district. 1. Hākim hāre aur muṅh hī muṅh māre . Prov. For the master's loss the servant pays. 2. Hākim do jānne-vāloṅ meṅ ek anjān . Prov. The judge is an ignorant man between two knowing ones (plaintiff and defendant). 2. The great ruler of the universe. 3. E. Landlord; zamīndār. hākim-i-bā-ikhtiyār, yā zī-ikhtiyār, yā majāz , G. G. A competent authority. hākim-i-bālā , A superior officer. hākim-i-dīvānī . An officer of the civil court; the civil power. hākim-i-zer-dast . An inferior or subordinate officer. hākim-i-adālat , A judge; an administrator of justice. hākim-i-ٴalā , The paramount power; highest authority. hākim-i-faujdārī , A judge who presides over a criminal court. hākim ke kutte…
(part. act. of حکم q. v.) s. m. A ruler, a governour, a judge, a commander, a master. (Plur. حکام ḥukkām, or Pers. حاکمان ḥākimān ). حاکم نشين ḥākim-nishīn, Seat of a ruler or sovereign.
subs: s.m. A judge, &c. with full powers; a competent authority
s.m. The civil power; an officer of the civil court
s.m. An inferior or subordinate officer
s.m. The highest authority; the paramount power
s.m. A judge who presides over a criminal court
s.m. Seat of a ruler or governor, &c.
s.m. The present ruler or rulers, the government of the day.

def: s.m. A wise man, a sage; a philosopher; — a physician, doctor
more: n. m. Pl. hukamā , حکمت science. 1. A philosopher; man of learning; a sage. 2. A physician; practitioner ( tabīb ). Hakīm ko qārūre se lāj! Prov. A physician! and ashamed to see his patient's urine! (He is ashamed of his profession). nīm-hakīm , n. m. A raw, inexperienced physician. Nīm hakīm, khatra-jān, N/?/m mullā, khatra-īmān . Prov. A physician half-skilled kills the body whole, The half-witted divine endangers the soul.
(from حکمت) adj. used substantively, A sage, a philosopher, a physician.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian حکیم (hakīm), from Arabic حَكِيم (ḥakīm). Compare Punjabi ਹਕੀਮ (hakīm) / حکیم (hakīm), Gujarati હકીમ (hakīm).

def: adj. & adv. In the manner of one having authority; judicial, official; — with authority; judicially, magisterially.
more: adj. lit . as one having authority. Judicial; official. hākimā'nah , adv. With authority; judicially; magisterially.

def: adj. & adv. Sage-like; philosophical; — physician-like

adj. Just, proper, right, correct, true; suitable to reality or fact (syn. ṭhīk); — s.m. Justness, propriety, rightness, correctness, truth; reality, fact; — justice; rectitude; — equity; — right, title, privilege, claim, due, lot, portion, share, proprietorship; — duty, obligation; — behalf, benefit, interest; — the Truth, the true God
more: adj. حق was right. Just; right; true ( ṭhīk , 15). 1. Haq kahe so mārā jāë . Prov. Tell the truth and be hanged. 2. Haq haq hai, nā-haq nā-haq hai . Prov. Truth is truth, falsehood is vain. 3. Haq kā sāthī Khudā . Prov. God is on the side of truth. 4. Haq kar, halāl kar, din meṅ hazār kar . Prov. Do right, be pure, a thousand female charms secure.

n. m. 1. Right; equity; justice; merits. 2. A right; privilege. 3. Right of action; charter; franchise; title. 4. Duty; obligation. Apnā haq adā kar dīyā . I have done my duty. 5. Dues; claims; a legal fee or perquisite. 6. Interest; advantage; concern; behalf. Mere haq meṅ duā karo . Pray for me. 7. Truth; the true God. 1. Haq nām Āllāh kā . Prov. God is truth. 2. Haq sab ko detā hai! God gives to all. 3. Paṛho beṭā miṭṭhū, haq Allāh, pāk zāt Allāh . Say, say my pretty Poll, praise be to God the true and pure. 4. Haq sab ko piyārā hai Truth is loved by all. 5. Haq kaṛvā hai . Prov. Truth is bitter. (A. Al haq o murrun .) 6. Haq to yeh hai . The truth is. haq adā k. , v. a. def: s.m. State, condition, circumstance, case, predicament, situation; existing or present state (as of revenue collections, &c.); a state of ecstasy, frenzy, or religious transport; — present time; (in Gram.) the present tense; — good condition, prosperous circumstances; — business, affair, matter, thing; statement, account, story, history; — adj. & adv. Present, current, now passing; — at present, &c. ( = ḥāl-meṅ, al-ḥāl, qq.v.)
more: n. m. حال condition. 1. State; condition; present or actual state. 1. Nahīṅ mālūm kaun hai woh chhināl, Kar dīyā mere lāl kā yeh hāl? Wom. Shauq. 2. Hāl gayā, ahvāl gayā, dil kā khiyāl na gayā . Prov. Health gone, wealth gone, the image in the mind not gone. 2. The present time; ( Gram .) Present tense ( bartmān ). 3. Situation; position; circumstances; affairs. 4. Statement; account; report ( kaifiyat ); case. 5. History; story; recital; narrative. 6. Life; vitality; stamina; strength. 1. Mujh meṅ ab hāl nahīṅ hai . 2. Sābit ho jo daglā, to nahīṅ mozoṅ meṅ huchh hāl, Tīroṅ meṅ hai par-gīrī to be-chiliah kamāṅ hai . (Satire on the king's Infantry). Saudā. If whole perchance his coat, the soldier's boots in sad case are, His arrow's feathers left mayhap, the place to fix it bare. 7. Extasy; frenzy; religious transport; the poet's phrensy. 1. Ātā hai unko dekh, mujarrad ke dil ko hāl, Raṅḍ…

def: n. m. S. हल A plough. 1. Tiryā rove purkh binā, khetī rove har binā . Prov. The wife without her husband weeps, the field unploughed all restless sleeps. 2. Ghar māṅ hal na baldiyā, māṅgeṅ ikhye haldiyā . Rus. Prov. Not even a plough nor oxen owneth he, he asks for sugarcane for his plough fee. 3. Lage halad aur hove balad . Prov. 1. Put to the plough, an ox become. 2. Turmeric applied (no sooner wed), she grew as fat as an ox. halā'ëtā, halwāṭ, harauṭī, halsot . The first ploughing of the season. It is generally preceded by the taking of omens, and other superstitious ceremonies. A commencement is made at the N. W. corner and facing the S. E., and the plough is then driven towards the middle of the field. Carnegy . hal-barār, hal-sārī , n. f. Assessment according to the number of ploughs. hal-bandī , n. f. A sharer in a plough. hal-sajjā aur jotā , n. The three shares in a plough, viz. the ploughman's and his two bullocks'. hal-ghasīṭ, har-ghasīṭ , n. f. adj. Legal, lawful, allowable, free, right, having religious sanction (opp. to ḥarām); (an animal) suitable for food, lawful to eat, killed as prescribed by law; — lawfully acquired or earned; — (a woman) laying aside mourning for the death of her husband at the expiration of one hundred days (during which time she is not by law allowed to marry)
more: Mah. adj. حل being lawful. Opp. of harām unlawful. Having religious sanction; (an animal) suitable for food or killed as prescribed by the ecclesiastical law; lawful to eat; lawfully earned. 1. Tīsre din murdār bhī halāl hai . Prov. After three days' fasting even a dead animal is lawful food. 2. Fāl kī kauṛiyāṅ mullā ko halāl . Prov. Divination's fee the divine may lawfully take. (Money earned is money lawfully got). 3. Pīyo, Chirkīṅ, sharāb, khāo kabāb, Ek harām, ek halāl hai, sāhib . Chirkīn. Drink wine and eat roast meat, Chirkīn, The lawful food condones the sin. 4. Lākhoṅ meṅ koī le hai muhabbat se haq kā nām, Varnā sab apne peṭ ke hīle hain, makr o dām, Nā āqbat se dhiyān, na rāh-i-Khudā se kām, Samjhe na kuchh halāl, na jānā ki hai harām, Jo jis se ho sakā so kīyā peṭ ke līye . Nazīr. Among a hundred thousand few do love the name they take, Else all are scheming, plotting, feignin…

(from حل) adj. 1. Legal, allowable, lawful, right. 2. (A woman) laying aside mourning for the death of her husband, at the expiration of 100 days (during which time she is not by law allowed to marry). حلال خور ḥalāl-ḵẖor, s. m. A person of the lowest cast, generally a sweeper, or employed in the meanest and dirtiest occupations (so called, because every thing is lawful food to him). حلال خوري ḥalāl-ḵẖor-ī, s. f. 1. The business or state of a sweeper, &c. 2. A female sweeper, &c. حلال کرنا ḥalāl karnā, 1. To make lawful; to slay according to the forms prescribed by the Muhammadan religion. 2. To murder. 3. To marry. ¶ ḥallāl, s. Resolver, untier.
subs: s.m. One who eats what is lawful; one whose earnings are legitimate; — a man of the lowest caste (who performs the lowest functions; so called because everything is lawful food to him); — a sweeper
s.f. The business or state of a ḥalāl-ḵẖor or sweeper; — a female sweeper
v.t. To make lawful; to slaughter (an animal) according to the forms prescribed by the Mohammadan law; — to kill, murder; — to marry a kept woman or mistress; to make an honest woman of

def: n. m. 1. One whose earnings are legitimate; one who eats what is lawful. 2. A man of the lowest caste who performs the lowest functions; a sweeper. halāl-khorī , n. f. A female sweeper. halāl kā , adj. Legitimate; lawful; pure. halāl k. , v. a. 1. To kill an animal for food according to the forms prescribed by the Mah. religion; to make lawful. 2. To kill; murder. 3. To be married to the woman one lives with. halāl kar-ke khānā , v. n. To do conscientiously the work for which one is paid. Jiske chār paise lo, unheṅ halāl kar-ke khāo . Prov. From whom four pice for food you take his work do and it lawful make. halālī, halāl-zādah , adj. 1. Born in wedlock; legitimate. 2. ( Ironic .) Wicked. 3. ( Ironic .) Bastard ( harāmī ). aulād-ul-halāl . Legitimate children.

( Gram .) The case of a noun ( kārak ). 5. Vertigo. 6. Religious frensy. hālat-i-izāfī , n. f. The genitive case. hālat-i-fāëlī , n. f. The nominative case. hālat-i-mafūlī , n. f. To interrupt; check; stop one who is speaking. halaq baiṭhnā , v. n. To become hoarse. halaq dabānā , v. a. To throttle; stifle; smother; strangle. halaq ke darbān , Wom. One's own children. def: adj. S. लघु light. Opp. of bhārī . See ادني and اوچها (1, 2, 4—7). 1. Light; not heavy. 2. Poor; not fertile. 3. Shallow. 4. Small. 5. Trifling. 6. Moderate; low. 7. Cheap; low-priced. Halke dāmoṅ kā . 8. Depressed (trade). 9. Easy; light. Halkā kām . 10. Ineffectual. 11. Insignificant. 12. Silly. 13. Mean; shabby. halke bhārī honā , v. n. 1. To be sick and tired of; to feel burdensome. Chār din ke vāste kyoṅ halke bhārī hote ho? 2. To lower or humiliate oneself. halkā-pan, halkā-panā , n. m. 1. Lightness. 2. Shallowness. 3. Insignificance. 4. Levity. 5. Meanness. halkā-phulkā, halkī-phulkī, halkā phūl , adj. Light as a feather. halkā raṅg , n. m. A light color. halkā k. , v. a. 1. To lighten (a load). 2. To abate; reduce; mitigate ( kam k . 1—4). 3. To lower (in one's eyes); disgrace. Unke sāmne mujhe halkā na karo! halkā khānā . Light food. halkā lahū , Thin blood. halkā mol lagānā , v. a. To put a low or moderate price upon. halkā h. , v. n. 1. To become or feel light. Nahāë se dehī halkī ho gaī . 2. To be abated, mitigated, etc. Bukhār halkā ho gayā . 3. To be freed from an obligation or responsibility. Unke bojh se halke hue . 4. To ease oneself. Halke ho lo, pīchhe chaleṅge! 5. To be ashamed, abashed. Nāhaq un se halkā honā paṛā . 6. ( Slang ) To have sexual intercourse with. kalkī bāt kahnā , To say something unworthy of oneself. halkī jamā . Light assessment. halkī choṭ . A slight hurt or wound. halkī chorī ; Ped. sarqa-i-khafīf , n. f. Petty theft. halke halke, haule haule ; Rus. halve halve ; Farrukh. haruge haruge , adv. Slowly ( sahaj meṅ , 1).

ety: Prk. हलुकअं, or लहुकअं; S. लघुक+कं
def: adj. (f. -ī), Light (in weight, or in character, or in colour, &c.); unimportant, of no consequence, of little moment; of little or low estimation, or authority, or influence; ineffectual; insignificant; frivolous, puerile; mean, pitiful, petty, scurvy, shabby, despicable, debased; silly; — small (as a measure, or capacity, &c.); weak; easy; poor (as soil, or as blood); shallow; mild, gentle, soft (as wind, heat, cold, rain, &c.); neap (tide); — faint, pale (as colour); soft (as water); — trifling, slight (matter, or work); manageable; easy of endurance; — light, easy of digestion (as food, or diet); of low price, or of little value; low, moderate; cheap; — depressed (trade)
more: adj. Light (in weight or character), debased, mean, silly, easy, soft, cheap. هلکاپن halkā-pan, s. m. Lightness, levity, vanity, despicableness. هلکا جاننا halkā jānnā, To disdain, to disesteem. هلکا کرنا halkā karnā, To lighten, exonerate, to ease, assuage, debase, abase, depreciate, affront.
subs: To be weary (of), to feel burdensome; — to lower or humble oneself
or halkā-phūl, adj. Exceedingly light, light as a feather
v.t. To think lightly or meanly of, to disesteem, despise, disdain, scorn
v.t. To lighten (a burden, &c.), assuage, ease, mitigate, alleviate, exonerate; — to abate, to reduce; — to lower (one, in the esteem of), to disgrace, to debase, to depreciate
(-meṅ), To put a low or moderate price (upon)
wety: Inherited from Sanskrit लघु (laghú).

ety: halkā, q.v. + āʼī = Prk. अइआ = S. ता+इका
def: s.f. Lightness, &c. (i.q. halkā-pan, q.v.). s.m. Lightness, littleness; shallowness; poorness; lowliness; insignificance; levity, frivolity, vanity; silliness; meanness, despicableness.
s. f. Lightness, levity.

adj. 1. Worried; harassed. Yeh pāoṅ ghisaṭ-kar chalne se kyoṅ raste ko hairān karo? Aur pople muṅh se roṭī ko mat mal mal-kar kalkān karo! Old age. Nazīr. Why drag along your feet and so the road distress? Why plague the bread your toothless gums oft press and press? 2. Confounded; confused.
adj. Harassed, worried; wearied, tired; — confounded, confused.
more: (perhaps from هلاک) adj. Confounded, confused, tired, wearied.
wety: Borrowed from Chagatai هلکان (halkān).

ety: halkā, q.v. + ā = āw = Prk. आव or आवे = S. आ (caus. aug.) + nā
def: v.t. To lighten (i.q. halkā karnā).
v.t. To cause to move; to spur on, to incite, to abet.

def: s.m. Lightness, littleness; shallowness; poorness; lowliness; insignificance; levity, frivolity, vanity; silliness; meanness, despicableness.

def: (dakh.) To gild ( Harris ).

(Heb. ) s. m. Solution, loosing. حلکاري ḥall-kārī, s. f. Gilding. حل کرنا ḥall karnā, To loosen, to solve, to dissolve, discuss, dilute. حل هونا hall honā, To be solved, loosened, resolved, dissolved, &c.

v.n. To be loosened, be opened, to be overcome (as difficulties); to be solved; to be resolved, be dissolved, &c.

ety: perhaps akin to halahalā (onomat.); cf. also Urdū ḥalla
def: s.m. Noise, tumult, outcry, shout, uproar (syn. hullar); attack, assault, onset (syn. ḥamla)
more: s. m. 1. Uproar, tumult. 2. Assault. ¶ hilā, 1. adj. Domesticated, tame. هلا ملا hilā-milā, Amicable, attached. 2. s. m. Slime, mud.
s. (dakh.) Name of an astringent nut, yellow or chebulic myrobalan: see S̱. هرا harrā.
subs: v.n. To make a noise; to shout, to raise an outcry; — to make an attack or assault (upon), to assault with a rush; — to work vigorously or with a will (at anything)
s.m. One who makes a noise; a noisy fellow; a turbulent man, a rioter
s.m. Noise, clamour; foolish talking, loquacity.

def: s.m. (dialec.) = harrā, or harlā, or haṛ, qq.v. s.m. (dialec.) = hallā, q.v. s.m. Attack, assault, onset, storm
s.m. = هلا hallā, 'attack,' q.v. s.m. Attack, assault, onset, storm

s. (dakh.) A kind of necklace: see نيمبولي nīmbolī.

ety: corr. of A. ḥamla?; but cf. ḥallā
def: s.m. Attack, assault, onset, storm
more: (perhaps for Arab. حمله) s. m. Assault, onset, attack, storm. حله کرنا ḥalla karnā, To assault, storm, attack.

def: n. m. S. हाद 1. ( hullaṛ ) Noise; uproar; tumult. 2. See بيدا and حمله 1. hallā yā hillā k. , v. a. 1. ( hullaṛ k .) To make a noise. 2. To assault with a rush ( hamla k ). 3. To work; follow a trade. Kar hallā jo bhare pallā . Rus. Prov. Work with a will, your lap you 'll till. hallā yā hullaṛ karnevālā , n. m. 1. One who makes a noise. 2. A rioter.
s.m. A comber, a carder of cotton.

def: s. m. Loquacity, foolish talking.
s.m. Untier: solver; resolver, &c.

ety: hāl˚ = Prk. हल्ल(इ) = S. व्हल्य(ते), pass. (used actively) of rt. व्हल्
def: v.n. To move, shake, tremble, vibrate; to be agitated; — (gerund) shaking, trembling; ague-fit, paroxysm (e.g. yah dawā mat khānā tap-ke hālne-meṅ).
more: v. n. To shake. See هلنا hilnā.

def: s.m. = hālim, q.v. (cf. jokhim and jokhoṅ).

s.m. (dialec.) A billow, a high wave
subs: v.n. A wave to strike or dash (against).

def: s.f. Hurry, bustle; confusion, perturbation (i.q. haṛbaṛī); — fussiness; — affability, politeness.
s.m. (dialec.) Myrobalan (i.q. harrā; harḍā, &c.).
s.m. A wave, billow, surge.

v.t. (dialec.) To shake; to toss up and down, to dandle, play with, amuse.

def: s.f. See s.v. hal, 'plough.' s.m. State, condition, circumstance, case, predicament, situation; existing or present state (as of revenue collections, &c.); a state of ecstasy, frenzy, or religious transport; — present time; (in Gram.) the present tense; — good condition, prosperous circumstances; — business, affair, matter, thing; statement, account, story, history; — adj. & adv. Present, current, now passing; — at present, &c. ( = ḥāl-meṅ, al-ḥāl, qq.v.)

ḥāl-sābiq, s.m. The former or past state or condition
s.m. The present or current year, this year
adj. 'In broken condition,' ruined, distressed, wretched
v.n. To get into a state of ecstasy or frenzy
adv. At the present time, at present; for the present, for the time being; — instantly, immediately
shikasta-ḥāl, adj. In straitened circumstances, badly off, &c. ( = ḥāl-shikasta).

def: s.m. Sweetmeat; a kind of pudding made of flour, ghī, and sugar, or of sūjī, ghī, sirup, dried cocoa-nut, and spices; — an electuary; anything soft and sweet (like ḥalwā); — sweet fruit, fruit
more: n. m. حلو being sweet. 1. A sweetmeat made of flour, ghī , and sugar, or of sūji, ghī, sirup , cocoa-nut, and spices. Hamārā hī halvā khāë! Mah. Wom. May you partake of my funeral feast! (May I be dead)! 2. An electuary. 3. Any thing as soft as halvā ; flummery; jelly. halvāë-be-dūd , Fruit naturally ripened on the tree. halvā māṅḍā , n. m. Sweetmeats and cakes. Murdā bahisht meṅ jāë yā dozakh meṅ, apne halve māṅḍe se kām . Prov. Your soul may go to Heaven or Hell, your funeral feast your priest loves well. halvā machchhī . Pomfret. halvā nikālnā , v. a. To beat to a mummy.
(from حلو) s. m. A kind of sweetmeat made of flour, ghī and sugar. حلوا سوهن ḥalwā-sohan, s. m. Name of a sweatmeat. حلوا نکالنا ḥalwā nikālnā, To beat severely.
s. A sweetmeat (made as before described; or, sūjī, ghī, sirup, khoprā and spices: Qanoon-e-islam ).

s.m. A kind of sweetmeat (somewhat like 'toffee,' — said to be so called after one Sohan Lāl)
ḥalwā-maććhī, s.f. A kind of fish, the pomfret, Stromateus argenteus

def: n. m. 1. A confectioner who makes halvā , etc. Halvāī kī dūkān, dādā jī kī fātehā (Pop. fātyāṅ )! Prov. The confectioner's shop, his grandfather's funeral cakes! (The survivor, who is too poor or too miserly to go to the expense of providing the sweets required for his offering, mentally dedicates the confectioner's whole stock to his deceased ancestor). 2. E. The tribe or caste to which the trade is confined.
(from حلوا) s. m. A confectioner.

conj. Also, even, same, likewise, in the same manner, equally. (In compos.) Together, with, mutual, mutually, similar, same, equal; as, هم آغوش ham-āg̠ẖosh, Embracing, locked in each otherʼs arms. هم آغوشي ham-āg̠ẖosh-ī, s. f. Embrace. هم آواز ham-āwāz, Of the same tone or voice, consonant; agreeing together; a comrade, companion. هم آوازي ham-āwāz-ī, s. f. Concord, accordance. هم آهنگ ham-āhang, Harmonious, concordant. هم بزم ham-bazm, Of the same society, associating together. هم بستر ham-bistar, Sleeping together, lying on the same bed, bedfellow. هم بستري ham-bistar-ī, s. f. The sleeping together. هم پا ham-pā, A companion, an attendant. هم پله ham-palla, adj. Of the same scale (or side); a partisan. هم پياله ham-piyāla, Pot-companion. هم پيشه ham-pesha, adj. Of the same trade, similar in practice. هم ترازو ham-tarāzū, adj. Of equal weight; equal, matched. هم جليس ham-jalīs, Fellow-s…

adj. & s.m. Of the same town; — a townsman, a fellow-citizen
adj. & s.m.f. 'Of the same milk'; uterine, foster; — uterine or foster-brother or sister
adj. & s.m. Of the same society; associated; — an associate, a companion
adj. & s.m. Of like or similar fortune; — a comrade
adj. Of the same age; coeval (i.q. ham-sin)
adj. & s.m. 'With equal reins'; riding alongside of (another); — an equal, a peer; an associate, a companion
adj. & s.m. Coeval, cotemporary; — joined, allied; — adopted (as a brother); united (by friendship); — a cotemporary; an adopted brother; — an intimate friend or companion
adj. & s.m. Keeping pace with; pari passu; — a companion; — an attendant; — a footman
adj. Bound by a common oath
adj. & s.m. Of (or one of) the same tribe or race; — fellow-tribesman
adj. & s.m. Of the same business or craft; — fellow-workman
s.m. A convivial or pot-companion; — a fellow-commoner
adj. Of the same business, trade, or profession
or ham-suḵẖan, adj. & s.m. Engaged in conversation; conversing together; — a converser; — intercourse
adj. & s.m. Embracing; locked in each other's arms (i.q. ham-āg̠osh); — embracer
adj. Of the same assembly or party, &c. (i.q. ham-ṣǒḥbat, q.v.)
adj. & s.m. Of the same religion or creed; — co-religionist
adj. Having the same (or a common) centre; — concentric
adj. & s.m. Of the same temper, disposition, or religion; like-tempered; — a co-religionist
or ham-maṣlaḥat, adj. & s.m. Of the same counsel; having the same object or end in view; confederated; — a confederate; an accomplice
adj. Of the same meaning, synonymous
adj. & s.m. Of the same school; — a school-fellow
adj. & s.m. Of the same name, cognominal; — a namesake
adj. Singing in concord or concert
adj. & s.m. & f. Sitting, or conversing, with (another); — one who sits or converses with (another); — a companion; — a playmate (syn. ham-jolī)
s.m. A friend, an intimate companion
s.m. (lit. 'of the same salt'), A messmate
adj. & s.m. Partaking of the same morsel; eating together; — a messmate; a chum (cf. ham-piyāla)
adj. Of equal weight; balanced (i.q. ham-palla, q.v.)
adj. & s.m. Of the same country; — a fellow-countryman; compatriot
s.f. The being of the same country.
ex: ہَم ہون٘گے کامْیاب۔
ham hoṅge kāmyāb.
we will be successful.
ہَم سے نا کَبھی جُدا ہونا۔
ham se nā kabhī judā honā.
Don't ever separate from us.
wety: Inherited from Old Hindi हम (hama), हंम (haṃma), from Apabhramsa अम्हे (amhe), from Prakrit 𑀅𑀫𑁆𑀳𑁂 (amhe), from Sanskrit अस्मे (asme).

ety: old P. & Pārsī hama; Pehl. hamāk; Zend hama, S. सम
def: adj. All, the whole; every; — everyone (syn. sab)
more: adj. All, the whole, every one. همه دان hama-dān, Knowing every thing, experienced, wise, all-wise, omniscient. همه داني hama-dān-ī, s. f. Omniscience.
subs: adv. With all (one's) might; — with all (one's) heart and soul
adj. All-knowing, omniscient; all-wise; — well-versed; experienced
adv. With all this; withal, notwithstanding all this; — whereas.

def: 1. We and you; we two; both of us. 2. Everybody. 3. ( Slang ) Tag rag and bob tail.

ety: abst. s. fr. همه hama, by euphonic change of final (silent
def: s.f. Totality, entirety; all, the whole.

adj. All; together. hama-tan , adv. With all one's heart and soul. hama-dāṅ , adj. 1. Knowing every thing; omniscient. 2. Well-versed. hamā-dānī , n. f. Omniscience. hamā sifat mausūf , adj. Possessing every good quality. bā-īṅ-hamā , Withal; whereas.
pron. (Braj) = همين hameṅ, q.v.

ety: pl. of حمالة ḥimālat or حميلة ḥamīlat, rt. حمل 'to bear or carry'
def: s.f. Suspensory thong, or cord, or shoulder-belt (of a sword, &c.); — a necklace of flowers or of rupees; — a small Qǒrʼān suspended to the neck (as an amulet)
subs: v.t. To sling across the shoulders (as a sword, or gun, &c.).

def: n. f. حمل bore. 1. A small Qurān suspended from the neck as an amulet. 2. (Pop. hamel .) A necklace of flowers, or rupees.
more: (from حمل) s. f. 1. A swordbelt hung from the shoulders. 2. A necklace of flowers. 3. A small ḳurʼān suspended to the neck as an amulet.

ety: for A. حماقة, inf. n. of حمق 'to be or become foolish,' &c.
def: s.f. Foolishness, folly, stupidity, fatuity.
more: n. f. حمق was foolish. Folly; stupidity.
(more correctly ḥamāḳat, from حمق) s. f. Folly, stupidity.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian حماقت (himāqat), from Arabic حَمَاقَة (ḥamāqa).

def: s.m. The sign Aries, the first sign of the Zodiac.
more: n. m. 1. (H. peṭ ) Pregnancy; the fœtus ; embryo; the child in the womb. 2. The first sign of the zodiac, Aries or the Ram (H. mekh ). hamal, kisī hāl, yā gāt se honā , v. n. To be pregnant ( pāoṅ bhārī h .). hamal kā girānā, isqāt-i-hamal , Causing miscarriage. wazai hamal , The birth of a child.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian حمل (haml), from Arabic حَمْل (ḥaml).

def: adj. Alike, equal, like, resembling; — coequal; cotemporary; — s.m. An equal, a peer; — a friend, confidant, companion, comrade, associate.
more: adj. Like, equal, resembling, cotemporary; a comrade, associate.

def: s.m. A pigeon; a dove.
wety: From Arabic حَمَّام (ḥammām).

def: s.m. corr. of hāwan, q.v.
subs: q.v. s.m. A mortar (for pounding in)
(corr. himām-dasta, imām-dasta), s.m. Pestle and mortar.

def: s.m. The heating plant, amcmum; Jamaica pepper; stone parsley.
prop. n. Name of Abraham's father; and of Pharaoh's minister.
more: s. Name of a brother of Abraham, also of Pharaohʼs minister.

def: pron. (dialec.) 1˚ = ham; — 2˚ = hamārā; — 3˚ = hameṅ, qq.v. prop. n. Ham (son of Noah).
more: pron. plur. (dakh.) We, of us, us: see S̱. هم ham.

def: adj. & adv. Identical; alike, resembling; — certainly, surely; — apparently; — again; the same thing repeated; ditto; as before; — immediately; all of a sudden; — s.m. A phantom, spectre; — imagination; opinion
more: 1. s. m. The opinion, imagination. 2. adj. Like, resembling. 3. adv. Immediately, suddenly. 4. Ditto, the same thing repeated, as before, again.

def: = H همنان हमनां hamanāṅ, or hamnāṅ, dialec. forms of the pron. hameṅ, q.v.
more: pron. plur. obj. case (dakh.) To us, us.

def: = H همنا हमना hamanā, or hamnā, dialec. forms of the pron. hameṅ, q.v.
more: pron. plur. (dakh.) See همن haman or همنا hamnā.

r: hamāndam, hamāngāh, ham, ān, dam, gāh
def: adv. That instant; at that very moment; — all at once, immediately, suddenly; — as soon as; or ever.

r: hamāngāh, hamāndam, ham, ān, dam, gāh
def: adv. That instant; at that very moment; — all at once, immediately, suddenly; — as soon as; or ever.
pron. (Bhojpuri) = ham, q.v.

pron. (rustic or dialec.), contrac. of next, q.v.
pron. (rustic or dialec.) = hamārā, q.v.

ety: Ap. Prk. अम्हारा; Prk. अम्हक्केरओ; S. अस्म+कृत+कः
def: adj. (gen. pl. of maiṅ, 'I'; — fem. hamārī), Of us; our; ours; — (often used for merā) my; mine.
more: pron. possess. plur. (of مين maiṅ ) Ourʼs, our, of us.
ex:
سارے جہاں سے اچھا ہندوستاں ہَمارا
ہم بلبلیں ہیں اس کی، یہ گلستاں ہمارا
sāre jahā̃ se acchā hindostā̃ hamārā
ham bulbulẽ ha͠i is kī, ye gulistā̃ hamārā

Better than the whole world, is our Hindustan;
We are its nightingales, and it is our flower-garden.
wety: Inherited from Old Hindi हमारा (hamārā), हंमारा (haṃmārā), from Apabhramsa अम्हार (amhāra) + Middle Indo-Aryan -𑀓- (-ka-) by metathesis; see there for more

def: pro. S. अस्माकम् Ours; our.
adj. (f. -ī) = hamārā, q.v.
pron. (Braj, or dialec.) = hamārā, q.v.
adj. = hamārā, q.v.
pron. We and you, we two; both of us; — everybody.

ety: Dakh.; prob. P. ham+ātam = S. आत्मा
def: Equality; rivalry, competition; contention (i.q. ham-sarī, q.v.).
more: m. s. (dakh.) Contest, competition, equality, rivalry: see هم سري ham-sar-ī, under P. هم ham.

ety: Dakh.; prob. P. ham+ātam = S. आत्मा
def: Equality; rivalry, competition; contention (i.q. ham-sarī, q.v.).
more: s. (dakh.) Contest, competition, equality, rivalry: see هم سري ham-sar-ī, under P. هم ham.

def: adv. (dialec.) Yes.
s.f. Praise (of God)
more: s. f. Praise. حمد کرنا ḥamd karnā, To praise or bless (God).
ex:
حمد و ثنا اُسی کو جو ذات جاودانی
ہمسر نہیں ہے اُس کا کوئی نہ کوئی ثانی

hamd o sanā usī ko jo zāt-i jāvidānī
hamsar nahī̃ hai us kā koī na koī sānī

All praise belongs to the Being, who is Eternal, Everlasting;
He has no associate, none is His equal.
حمد اور نعت لکھنا ہماری شاعری کی ایک ممتاز اور اہم روایت ہے۔
mere āqā
The composition of hymns and naats is a prominent and important tradition, part of our poetry.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian حمد (hamd), taken from Arabic حَمْد (ḥamd).

ety: for A. همدة hamdat, v.n. fr. همد 'to be extinguished; to be dead,' &c.
def: s.m. Apoplexy.
s.f. A sort of necklace (generally made of rupees, and furnished with a pendant; — cf. haikal).
s.f. formed by imāla fr. ḥamāʼil, q.v.
pron. object. Pl. of mujhe ; Pr. amhe . To us; us.

ety: Ap. Prk. अम्हहिं or अम्हहं, fr. Prk. अहि or अमअ = Vedic S. अस्मे
def: pron. (dat. & acc. of ham, q.v.), To us; us.
more: pron. plur. in the objective case, Us, to us: (or in dakh. nom.) we. ¶ ham-īṅ, We ourselves.

def: adv. contrac. of hamesha, q.v.
adv. Always, ever, continually, perpetually, incessantly
more: adv. Always, ever, continually, perpetually. هميشه کا hamesha kā, Eternal.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian هَمیشَه (hamēša).

def: s.f. Eternity, perpetuity.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian همیشگی (hamēšagī). Equivalent to ہمیشہ (hameśa, “forever”) + ـگی (-gī).

def: adv. Always; incessantly ( sadā ). hameshgī , n. f. Perpetuity; eternity.
s.m. Egotism, vanity, conceit, pride, arrogance.
more: s. m. Vanity, egotism, arrogance, pride.

ety: act. part. n. of حمي 'to protect,' &c.
def: s.m. Protector, defender; supporter, patron; — s.f. Assurance, confirmation or ratification (to an engagement) afforded by a third person's pledging himself to the fulfilment of it
more: n. m. حمي he protected it. A protector; supporter; patron.

(part. act. from حما or حمايت) s. m. A protector, a defender. حامي بهرنا ḥāmī bharnā, To believe, acknowledge, confess, allow, own, consent. Saudā says: نازبرادري کي حامي تب بهرون دل کي کمر دم دلا سادي بندها لون استقامت کي ليي Nāz-bardārī kī ḥāmī tab bharūṅ, dil kī kamar dam-dilāsā de bandhā lūṅ, istiḳāmat ke liye, I will then consent to endure her capricious airs, when by soft persuasion I have fortified my heart for perseverance.
subs: (-kī), To give a pledge or assurance, to pledge oneself (to), to agree, promise, consent, allow, own, acknowledge, confess
s.m. One who gives assurance or satisfaction by pledging himself to the fulfilment of the engagement, &c. entered into by another; — an assurance.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian حَامِی (hāmī), borrowed from Arabic حامٍ (ḥāmin). First attested in c. 1672 as Middle Hindi حامی (ḥamy /⁠hāmī⁠/).

def: n. f. Yes. hāmī bharnā ; Sant. heng , v. n. See iqrār k . 1. To say yes. 2. To confirm.
See حامي بهرنا ḥāmī bharnā, under حامي ḥāmī.

ety: said to be corr. fr. A. ḥāmī, q.v.; but prob. akin to hāṅ, q.v.
def: s.f. Confirmation, ratification (by a third person); assurance, guarantee; assent
subs: (-kī), To afford confirmation (of), to confirm; to pledge oneself (to the fulfilment of); to give assurance (of); to promise, to assent.

def: s.m. One who praises (God).
more: (part. act. of حمد) s. m. A praiser (peculiarly, of God).

ety: ḥamīda, for A. حميدة ḥamīdat, fem. of ḥamīd, fr. حمد 'to praise'
def: adj. Praised, commended, approved; praiseworthy, laudable, commendable, approvable
more: m. (from حمد) adj. 1. Praised. 2. Laudable, glorious. اخلاق حميده aḵẖlāḳ-i-ḥamīda, Laudable qualities or manners. اوصاف حميده auṣāf-i-ḥamīda, Laudable qualifications.
subs: Laudable qualities, praiseworthy manners or disposition

ety: ḥamīda, for A. حميدة ḥamīdat, fem. of ḥamīd, fr. حمد 'to praise'
def: adj. Praised, commended, approved; praiseworthy, laudable, commendable, approvable
more: f. (from حمد) adj. 1. Praised. 2. Laudable, glorious. اخلاق حميده aḵẖlāḳ-i-ḥamīda, Laudable qualities or manners. اوصاف حميده auṣāf-i-ḥamīda, Laudable qualifications.
subs: Laudable qualities, praiseworthy manners or disposition

ety: act. part. n. of حمل 'to bear or carry'
def: s.m. A bearer, carrier, porter; — adj. Pregnant ( = ḥāmila, q.v.)
more: n. m. حمل bore. ( In Comp .) A carrier. hāmil-i-khat, yā ruqqāh . The bearer of a letter or cheque. Nāmah to kaī din se likhā rakkhā thā, lekin Is rashk se kartā na thā qāsid ke havāle, Yeh dīda-i-nā dīdah raheṅ dīd se mahrūm, Aur hāmil-i-khat āṅkh rukh-i-yār pe ḍāle! Jurat. A letter I wrote days ago, but jealousy To send it by a messenger would not let me, To think the bearer of my letter should see thee, While for these longing eyes this pleasure should not be.
(part. act. of حمل Heb. ) 1. s. m. A carrier, porter, bearer. 2. adj. f. Pregnant (a woman). حامل متن ḥāmil-i-matan, A commentary rehearsing, or annexed to, the text.
subs: s.m. A commentary rehearsing, or annexed to, the text.

def: (fr. حمل 'to carry'), s.m. lit. 'What is carried, or carried off or away'; rubbish, rotten leaves or sticks, scum, &c. (carried down by a stream); — a foundling, a bastard, child of a whore; — a surety.
more: (from حمل) s. m. 1. A son of a whore, a bastard. 2. A surety. 3. Herbs, sticks, &c. carried down by the stream.

ety: for A. حاملة ḥāmilat, fem. of ḥāmil
def: v.n. To be or become pregnant, to conceive.
more: adj. حمل carrying. Carrying; pregnant; being with child (H. peṭ se ).
(from حمل) adj. f. Pregnant. حامله هونا ḥāmila honā, To conceive, to be pregnant.

def: adj. & adv. Even this, this very, this same; — equally, in the same manner; — only, solely, neither more nor less.

ety: ham, q.v.+emphat. part. īṅ = ī = hī, q.v.
def: pron. We ourselves; we even, even we.

ety: for A. حامية ḥāmīyat, fem. of ḥāmī
def: s.f. Protectress, &c.; a protective body.
more: s. f. (of حامي) Protectress, female defender.
